title: 177 Mathematics Fig. 3
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - cardboard as shown in Fig
objective:
To verify that when two lines intersect, vertically opposite angles
are equal
materials:
Drawing sheet, thumb pins, colour pencil, tracing
paper, adhesive, cardboard.
procedure:
1.   Take a cardboard of convenient size and paste a white sheet on it.
2.   Draw a pair of intersecting lines AB and CD as shown in Fig. 1.
Fig. 1
3.   Mark the point of intersection P of these lines.
4.   Colour   ∠ BPC and   ∠ APD with the same colour, say yellow.
5.   Colour   ∠ BPD and   ∠ APC with the same colour, say green.
observation:
1.   Δ   PQR and   Δ   ABC are __________ triangles.
2.   Area of   Δ   PQR = __________ squares.
Area of   Δ   ABC = __________ squares.
Area of   Δ   PQR = Area of   Δ   _______.
So, congruent triangles have _________ area.
3.   Area of   Δ   RST = __________ squares.
Area of   Δ   XYZ = __________squares.
So, area of   Δ   RST = area of   Δ   _______.
4.   Δ   RST and   Δ   XYZ do not cover each other _______.
Δ   RST and   Δ   XYZ are not _______.
Thus, triangles having equal area may not be congruent.
discussion:
This activity can be used to explain relationship between congruency and
areas of different geometric shapes.
184 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
